title: Tips to keep your animals safe this New Year's Eve
description: MOSSEL BAY NEWS - The Garden Route SPCA...
bodyText:
[{"type":"paragraph","text":"MOSSEL BAY NEWS - The Garden Route SPCA Mossel Bay branch has urged pet owners and animal lovers to take extra precautions to keep animals safe this New Year's Eve.","html":"MOSSEL BAY NEWS - The Garden Route SPCA Mossel Bay branch has urged pet owners and animal lovers to take extra precautions to keep animals safe this New Year's Eve."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"\"The festive season is a time of celebration, but it can be a frightening and stressful time for our animals,\" said the Garden Route SPCA Mossel Bay branch manager, Elize Goosen."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"Cats and dogs can hear a much wider range of sounds than humans, including very soft and high-pitched noises that we cannot detect. Because of this heightened sensitivity, the loud, sudden bangs and flashes from fireworks can be absolutely terrifying for them,\" she said.","html":"\"Cats and dogs can hear a much wider range of sounds than humans, including very soft and high-pitched noises that we cannot detect. Because of this heightened sensitivity, the loud, sudden bangs and flashes from fireworks can be absolutely terrifying for them,\" she said."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Goosen said fireworks can cause animals to panic, try to escape, injure themselves, or become lost.","html":"Goosen said fireworks can cause animals to panic, try to escape, injure themselves, or become lost."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"As responsible pet owners and animal lovers, it is our duty to take extra precautions to keep them safe and calm during this time.\"","html":"\"As responsible pet owners and animal lovers, it is our duty to take extra precautions to keep them safe and calm during this time.\""},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Goosen gave the following tips to keep your animals safe this evening:• Ensure proper identification: Make sure your pets have up-to-date identification, including a microchip, collar, and tags with your current contact details.• Stay home if possible: If you expect fireworks in your area, stay home with your animals to comfort and reassure them.• Keep them safely indoors: Secure doors, windows, and gates to prevent frightened animals from escaping.• Reduce noise and visual stimulation: Close curtains and windows to lessen flashes and sound. Playing calming or background music can help drown out the noise of fireworks.• Create a safe, comfortable space: Provide familiar blankets, toys, or allow them to stay in their usual safe space where they feel secure.• Seek professional advice if needed: If you know your animal reacts severely to fireworks, consult your veterinarian in advance for professional guidance or calming solutions.","html":"Goosen gave the following tips to keep your animals safe this evening:
• Ensure proper identification: Make sure your pets have up-to-date identification, including a microchip, collar, and tags with your current contact details.
• Stay home if possible: If you expect fireworks in your area, stay home with your animals to comfort and reassure them.
• Keep them safely indoors: Secure doors, windows, and gates to prevent frightened animals from escaping.
• Reduce noise and visual stimulation: Close curtains and windows to lessen flashes and sound. Playing calming or background music can help drown out the noise of fireworks.
• Create a safe, comfortable space: Provide familiar blankets, toys, or allow them to stay in their usual safe space where they feel secure.
• Seek professional advice if needed: If you know your animal reacts severely to fireworks, consult your veterinarian in advance for professional guidance or calming solutions."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"\"Let’s celebrate responsibly and remember to protect the animals who depend on us for their safety and well-being,\" said Goosen.","html":"\"Let’s celebrate responsibly and remember to protect the animals who depend on us for their safety and well-being,\" said Goosen."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"She said that if there are any emergencies, the SPCA can be contacted on 072 287 1761.","html":"She said that if there are any emergencies, the SPCA can be contacted on 072 287 1761."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]
